pages.raw_text:
A letter has been received by
the stockholders OF the Idaho
Smelling & Refining Co, signed by
j. Herbert Anderson, the president
of the corporation, reading as fol
lows:


AS you are no doubt aware, on
the application of certain large
creditors. receiver was appointed
i'll connection with the affairs of
the Idaho Smelling & Refining Co.
on March 6 of this year in the
state of Idaho. Later temporary
receiver was appointed in the state
OF Washington. Later again, on the
petition of three creditors, the COM
pany went into bankruptcy and re-
manned in bankruptcy until Novem-
Der 2. The writer has been able
to arrange with the creditors to &C
cept bonds of the corporation for
their liabilities, thus clearing the
books of all pressing accounts and
giving the company 10 years with
In which to pay out.


THE writer has also been able
to interest capital to the extent of
100000 as an operating fund for
the smelter, which is more money
than has ever been invested in this
company since it was first organ
lied, and when this money will be
used solely for the. purpose of OPP
Grating and not to reduce liabilities
